# Let's read the file to inspect
path = "/mnt/data/file-QZDcTMjhz7JvJY7DX8VuKp-text_to_analyse.txt"
try:
    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        text = f.read()
    print(text[:2000])
    print("Length:", len(text))
except Exception as e:
    print("Error:", e)
    text = ""

import re

text = text  # from earlier cell
spans = []

# 1) Ayahs
# We'll pattern search for ayah within braces after 'قال الله تعالى في القرآن:'
pattern = r'قال الله تعالى في القرآن:\s*\{([^}]*)\}[^[]*\[(?:[^\]]+)\]'
# Use re.DOTALL to capture across lines
for m in re.finditer(r'قال الله تعالى في القرآن:\s*\{([^}]*)\}[^[]*\[', text, flags=re.S):
    matn = m.group(1)
    # We need to trim edge spaces etc (spaces/newlines)
    span_text = matn.strip()
    # We should further trim using the rule: remove leading/trailing punctuation as edges
    # The edge removal list includes spaces and punctuation; We'll implement iteratively
    def trim_edge(s):
        # allowed to remove whitespace, newlines, tabs
        left = 0
        right = len(s)
        # remove leading/trailing whitespace
        while left < right and s[left] in " \t\r\n":
            left += 1
        while right > left and s[right-1] in " \t\r\n":
            right -= 1
        # Then remove leading punctuation marks: "«»'\"[](){}<>،؛:.,!?…ـ" as per rule
        punct = "«»'\"()[]{}<>،؛:.,!؟؟…ـ"
        while left < right and s[left] in punct:
            left += 1
        while right > left and s[right-1] in punct:
            right -= 1
        return s[left:right]
    span_text_trim = trim_edge(span_text)
    # ensure the actual substring equals the trimmed text if we slice from m
    # But the raw braces include the content; We'll compute start using the actual substring in text
    start = text.find(span_text_trim, m.start(1))
    # If not found, fallback to m.start(1)
    if start == -1:
        start = m.start(1)
        end = start + len(span_text_trim)
    else:
        end = start + len(span_text_trim)
    spans.append({"span_start": start, "span_end": end, "span_text": span_text_trim, "citation_type": "Ayah"})
    
# 2) Another ayah: There are 3 patterns of ayah after 'قال الله تعالى:'
# The above pattern might catch all; Let's print number of ayah spans
len(spans), spans[:3]

# Let's reparse for ayahs including "وقال الله تعالى" too
text = text  # from earlier
ayh_spans = []

def extract_ayahs_from_pattern(pattern_regex):
    import re
    res = []
    for m in re.finditer(pattern_regex, text, flags=re.S):
        matn = m.group(1)
        span_text = matn.strip()
        # trim edges
        def trim_edge(s):
            left = 0
            right = len(s)
            while left < right and s[left] in " \t\r\n":
                left += 1
            while right > left and s[right-1] in " \t\r\n":
                right -= 1
            punct = "«»'\"()[]{}<>،؛:.,!؟…،.\u0640"
            # Note: We included typical punctuation; We'll filter common ones
            while left < right and s[left] in punct:
                left += 1
            while right > left and s[right-1] in punct:
                right -= 1
            return s[left:right]
        span_text_trim = trim_edge(span_text)
        # locate in raw text
        start = text.find(span_text_trim)
        if start == -1:
            # fallback search around m.start(1)
            start = m.start(1)
        end = start + len(span_text_trim)
        res.append({"span_start": start, "span_end": end, "span_text": span_text_trim, "citation_type": "Ayah"})
    return res

pattern1 = r'قال الله تعالى في القرآن:\s*\{([^}]*)\}[^[]*\[([^\]]+)\]'
pattern2 = r'وقال الله تعالى:\s*\{([^}]*)\}[^[]*\[([^\]]+)\]'
# We'll call both
ayh_spans += extract_ayahs_from_pattern(pattern1)
ayh_spans += extract_ayahs_from_pattern(pattern2)
ayh_spans

# Extract hadith spans: using pattern for quotes after "وعن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم، قال:"
import re
hadith_spans = []
# We'll define a helper to trim edges
def trim_edges_arabic(s):
    left = 0
    right = len(s)
    while left < right and s[left] in " \t\r\n":
        left += 1
    while right > left and s[right-1] in " \t\r\n":
        right -= 1
    punct = "«»'\"()[]{}<>،؛:.,!؟…-ـ"
    while left < right and s[left] in punct:
        left += 1
    while right > left and s[right-1] in punct:
        right -= 1
    return s[left:right]
for m in re.finditer(r'وعن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم، قال:\s*"([^"]*)"', text, flags=re.S):
    matn = m.group(1)
    # trim edges
    span_text = trim_edges_arabic(matn)
    # Now compute the start index of this span_text in the raw string
    # Might be multiple occurrences; We'll search for the exact substring near m.start(1)
    start = text.find(span_text, m.start())
    if start == -1:
        start = m.start(1)
    end = start + len(span_text)
    hadith_spans.append({"span_start": start, "span_end": end, "span_text": span_text, "citation_type": "Hadith"})
hadith_spans[:5], len(hadith_spans)

for h in hadith_spans:
    print(h['span_text'], " | start=", h['span_start'], " end=", h['span_end'])

# Build final list
final_spans = []
# Use ayh_spans computed earlier
for a in ayh_spans:
    final_spans.append({
        "span_start": a["span_start"],
        "span_end": a["span_end"],
        "span_text": a["span_text"],
        "citation_type": "Ayah"
    })
# Add hadith spans
for h in hadith_spans:
    final_spans.append({
        "span_start": h["span_start"],
        "span_end": h["span_end"],
        "span_text": h["span_text"],
        "citation_type": "Hadith"
    })
# Sort by span_start
final_spans_sorted = sorted(final_spans, key=lambda x: x["span_start"])
len(final_spans_sorted), final_spans_sorted[:3]

# Append hadiths after ayahs
for h in hadith_spans:
    final_spans_sorted.append({
        "span_start": h["span_start"],
        "span_end": h["span_end"],
        "span_text": h["span_text"],
        "citation_type": "Hadith"
    })
# Sort again
final_spans_sorted = sorted(final_spans_sorted, key=lambda x: x["span_start"])
len(final_spans_sorted)

# Recompute final spans correctly
final_spans = []
# Add ayah spans
for a in ayh_spans:
    final_spans.append({"span_start": a["span_start"], "span_end": a["span_end"], "span_text": a["span_text"], "citation_type": "Ayah"})
# Add hadith spans
for h in hadith_spans:
    final_spans.append({"span_start": h["span_start"], "span_end": h["span_end"], "span_text": h["span_text"], "citation_type": "Hadith"})

final_spans_sorted = sorted(final_spans, key=lambda x: x["span_start"])
len(final_spans_sorted), final_spans_sorted

# Verify for each final span that the substring equals the span_text
ok = True
for s in final_spans_sorted:
    st = s["span_start"]; en = s["span_end"]; t = s["span_text"]
    if text[st:en] != t:
        print("Mismatch at", st, en, "Expected:", t, "But got:", text[st:en])
        ok = False
print("All matches?", ok)

content.text: {"citations":[{"span_start":222,"span_end":421,"span_text":"وَكَذَلَكَ أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ رُوحًا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا مَا كُنتَ تَدْرِي مَا الْكِتَابُ وَلَا الْإِيمَانُ وَلَكِنْ جَعَلْنَاهُ تَفْمِيَةً عَلَى قَلْبِكَ وَمُصَدِّقًا لِذِكْرِنَا وَهُدًى لِلْمُتَّقِينَ","citation_type":"Aya"},{"span_start":454,"span_end":593,"span_text":"وَإِنْ يَمُدُّوكَ عَلَى الْقَتْلِ ثَمَّ قَدْ يَمُدُّ اللَّهُ عِنْدَهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَلَيُدْخِلَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ غَلِيٍّ","citation_type":"Aya"},{"span_start":628,"span_end":685,"span_text":"وَإِنَّ هَذَا قَوْلُنَا لَمُبَيِّنٌ لِأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ","citation_type":"Aya"},{"span_start":735,"span_end":881,"span_text":"إِنَّما الأَعْمَالُ بِالنِيَّةِ، وَإِنَّمَا لِلسَّائِلِ نِعْمَةٌ أَوْ عَذَابٌ يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِ أَوْ يُصَلِّي لَهُ أَوْ يُصَدِّقَهُ أَوْ يُزَكِّيهِ","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":931,"span_end":984,"span_text":"مَنْ أَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ وَأَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ فَهَلَكَ","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":1034,"span_end":1135,"span_text":"مَنْ أَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ وَأَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ فَذَلِكَ الَّذِي أَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ وَأَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":1188,"span_end":1291,"span_text":"مَنْ أَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ وَأَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ فَكَذَلِكَ الَّذِي أَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ وَأَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":1341,"span_end":1444,"span_text":"مَنْ أَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ وَأَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ فَكَذَلِكَ الَّذِي أَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ وَأَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":1497,"span_end":1600,"span_text":"مَنْ أَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ وَأَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ فَكَذَلِكَ الَّذِي أَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ وَأَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":1650,"span_end":1753,"span_text":"مَنْ أَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ وَأَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ فَكَذَلِكَ الَّذِي أَخَذَ بِالنَفْسِ وَأَخَذَ بِالْقُرْآنِ","citation_type":"Hadeeth"}]}
